skill.name: longbridge
skill.description: Longbridge helps AI assistants work with Longbridge market data, account data, trading workflows, SDKs, CLI commands, MCP setup, and developer documentation.
skill.summary: Use Longbridge data, trading tools, SDKs, CLI guidance, and MCP workflows from AI assistants.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found many command, network, filesystem, and script patterns. Most Ruby backtick, weak-crypto, and dynamic-import alerts are false positives from Markdown code examples, but the skill does include remote installer commands that pipe downloaded content into shells and capabilities for account and order operations. No evidence of malicious prompt injection was found, but the skill should require publisher review and user warnings before publication.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Scripts Piped to Shells
high_findings.description: The setup and CLI references recommend curl-to-sh and PowerShell iwr-to-iex installation patterns. These are official Longbridge installation examples, but they execute downloaded content directly and are dangerous if a URL, network path, or agent instruction is compromised.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell commands are present in Markdown installation instructions. The context appears legitimate, but the execution pattern is inherently high risk for AI-assisted environments.

medium_findings.title: Trading and Account Operations Need Explicit User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill covers account balances, positions, private order updates, and order submission through CLI, SDK, and MCP workflows. This is expected for a brokerage integration, but accidental or overbroad AI execution could expose private financial data or initiate trades.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly document trading and account-data operations, and MCP guidance already warns to confirm order placement. No malicious intent is evident, but the capability has high user impact.

medium_findings.title: Local OAuth Token Cache Paths Are Documented
medium_findings.description: The references describe OAuth token storage under hidden Longbridge directories. This is normal credential caching, but agents should avoid reading, printing, committing, or exfiltrating these files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden token-cache paths are explicitly documented. This is legitimate OAuth behavior, but the files contain sensitive authorization material if accessed outside normal client flows.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Based Credentials Are Supported
medium_findings.description: The Go and Python SDK documentation supports configuration through environment variables, including app secrets and access tokens. This is common for SDKs, but generated code must avoid logging or hardcoding these values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly references environment-based configuration and credential names. This is standard SDK behavior, but it creates a secret-handling obligation for users and agents.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported hundreds of Ruby or shell backtick detections. Review found Markdown code fences, inline command names, and SDK snippets rather than Ruby backtick execution in a runnable skill program.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown documentation and examples, not executable Ruby source. Some commands are still operational if copied or run, which is covered by separate findings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives From Finance Terms
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections map to market suffixes, enum names, words such as Trade, and ticker-related documentation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or cryptographic verification logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain market and SDK terminology, not cryptographic algorithm calls. No implementation code for hashing or encryption was found in these locations.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Alerts Are Language Import Examples
low_findings.description: Dynamic import findings appear in SDK documentation import blocks for Go and Python. They are illustrative examples and not JavaScript dynamic import expressions executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows static import syntax inside Markdown examples. No JavaScript runtime code or dynamic module loading behavior was identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Install Scripts Piped to Shells
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup and CLI references recommend curl-to-sh and PowerShell iwr-to-iex installation patterns. These are official Longbridge installation examples, but they execute downloaded content directly and are dangerous if a URL, network path, or agent instruction is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell commands are present in Markdown installation instructions. The context appears legitimate, but the execution pattern is inherently high risk for AI-assisted environments.

dangerous_patterns.description: The skill covers account balances, positions, private order updates, and order submission through CLI, SDK, and MCP workflows. This is expected for a brokerage integration, but accidental or overbroad AI execution could expose private financial data or initiate trades.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly document trading and account-data operations, and MCP guidance already warns to confirm order placement. No malicious intent is evident, but the capability has high user impact.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local OAuth Token Cache Paths Are Documented
dangerous_patterns.description: The references describe OAuth token storage under hidden Longbridge directories. This is normal credential caching, but agents should avoid reading, printing, committing, or exfiltrating these files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden token-cache paths are explicitly documented. This is legitimate OAuth behavior, but the files contain sensitive authorization material if accessed outside normal client flows.

content.value_statement: Financial analysis needs current market data, account context, and clear trading safeguards. This skill guides AI assistants through Longbridge CLI, SDK, MCP, and documentation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Longbridge CLI, MCP, Python SDK, Rust SDK, or Go SDK.
Provides symbol format guidance for US, Hong Kong, China, Singapore, and crypto markets.
Guides quote, K-line, intraday, news, filing, fundamentals, and market sentiment lookups.
Documents account, positions, balances, orders, deposits, withdrawals, and portfolio commands.
Supports developer workflows for OAuth setup, SDK contexts, subscriptions, and API documentation.
Points AI tools to Longbridge Markdown documentation and MCP setup references.
limitations: It depends on Longbridge CLI, SDK, MCP, or website availability for live data.
Trading and account features require user authorization and appropriate Longbridge account permissions.
Some examples include remote install commands that should be reviewed before execution.
It is documentation guidance, not independent investment advice or a compliance review.

use_cases.title: Market Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Collect current quotes, news, filings, valuation data, and analyst estimates for a stock before making a personal decision.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Review Workflow
use_cases.description: Review positions, cash, margin, P&L, order status, and recent account activity with explicit confirmation for trading actions.

use_cases.title: Trading API Integration
use_cases.description: Build scripts or services with Longbridge Python, Rust, Go, CLI, or MCP references for market and trade operations.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Stock
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge data to summarize the latest quote, recent news, and basic valuation context for AAPL.US.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Portfolio
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge account tools to summarize my positions, cash, margin status, and largest unrealized gains or losses. Do not place orders.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Catalysts
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare TSLA.US and NVDA.US using Longbridge quotes, filings, news, analyst estimates, and valuation ranks. Explain the key drivers.

prompt_templates.title: Design an SDK Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Longbridge Python SDK workflow that fetches quotes, subscribes to updates, reviews orders, and requires confirmation before trades.

output_examples.output: A concise market brief with latest price direction, volume context, Longbridge news catalysts, filing references, and clear uncertainty notes.

output_examples.input: Show my portfolio risk using Longbridge.
output_examples.output: A portfolio summary covering positions, cash, margin indicators, concentration risks, and suggested checks before any transaction.

output_examples.input: Help me connect Longbridge MCP to my AI tool.
output_examples.output: Step-by-step setup guidance with OAuth expectations, scope review, revocation advice, and confirmation rules for order placement.

best_practices: Confirm every order, cancellation, replacement, or withdrawal action with the user before execution.
Prefer OAuth flows and least-privilege scopes instead of hardcoded API keys or access tokens.
Review remote installer commands before running them, especially pipe-to-shell commands.
anti_patterns: Do not execute trading commands from generated analysis without explicit user approval.
Do not print, store, or commit OAuth tokens, app secrets, access tokens, or token-cache files.
Do not treat Longbridge market output as guaranteed investment advice or regulatory guidance.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants use Longbridge market data, account tools, SDK references, CLI commands, MCP setup, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it place trades?
faq.answer: The referenced Longbridge tools support trading operations, but users should require explicit confirmation before any order action.

faq.question: Does it need Longbridge credentials?
faq.answer: Market data may vary by tool, but account and trading workflows require Longbridge authorization through OAuth or configured credentials.

faq.question: Which markets are covered?
faq.answer: The skill documents US, Hong Kong, China Shanghai, China Shenzhen, Singapore, and crypto symbol formats.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish automatically?
faq.answer: No. It includes high-risk install patterns and brokerage operations, so it should receive marketplace review before publication.

faq.question: Does it replace financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It organizes Longbridge data and workflows, but users remain responsible for investment, tax, and compliance decisions.
